title: Concrete Beam Height & Width Calculator 2026 | Hacé Cuentas
description: Calculate concrete beam height and width based on free span. Quick, free, and accurate sizing for your construction project.
intro: Concrete construction requires precise calculations: material quantities, concrete mix design, coverage rates, and unit costs. Common mistakes lead to material waste, shortages, or structural failures. This calculator determines the optimal height and width of a concrete beam based on its clear span. Values are current as of 2026 and reviewed regularly to keep you informed.
keyTakeaway: Formula: **h ≈ L/12, b = h/2**. Direct calculation with your measurements. Determine concrete beam height and width based on free span length.
useCases: Technical calculations for concrete beam sizing
Teaching and learning structural design concepts
explanation:
**Construction** projects require technical calculations: concrete, steel reinforcement, masonry, finishing, and waterproofing. Building codes and standards ensure consistent quality and safety.

## How the Calculation Works

This calculator uses the L/12 rule to estimate beam depth, then applies the b = h/2 ratio to determine width. It validates your inputs against typical ranges and alerts you to unusual values that may require professional review.

## Important Notes

This calculation is a reference estimate only. For critical decisions (structural design, large spans, unusual loads), consult with a licensed structural engineer. Information is current as of 2026 and updated periodically.

faq.q: What does the L/12 rule mean?
faq.a: It's a quick estimation rule: beam depth ≈ span length ÷ 12. Actual design requires load calculations and structural analysis.

faq.q: How do I know if my beam is the right size?
faq.a: Check deflection limits, shear capacity, and moment capacity with a structural engineer, especially for spans over 6–8 meters.

faq.q: What reinforcement does the beam need?
faq.a: Typically #4 to #5 bars lengthwise, with #3 stirrups spaced 6–10 inches apart. Consult a structural engineer for your specific project.

faq.q: What concrete strength should I use?
faq.a: 3000 psi (21 MPa) minimum for most residential work. Larger spans or heavy loads may require 4000 psi (28 MPa) or higher.

faq.q: Does span type matter (simply supported vs. continuous)?
faq.a: Yes. Continuous beams can be shallower than simply supported beams for the same span. This calculator assumes simply supported.

faq.q: Is this calculator free to use?
faq.a: Yes. All our calculators are free to use with no registration required.
Results are estimates based on industry standard formulas. For critical applications, always verify with a professional engineer.
No. All calculations run locally in your browser. We never send your data to any server.
